title: Network Evolution
summary: A concise narrative of why confidential compute systems evolved beyond simple public-state blockchains and isolated trusted operators.

bodySections.title: From Transparency to Selective Secrecy
bodySections.body:
Public chains are strong at shared verification, but weak at handling private state natively. That gap pushed teams toward patterns where sensitive inputs were hidden off-chain or trusted to centralized operators.

Arcium sits in the lineage of systems trying to preserve verifiability while reducing information leakage.

bodySections.title: Design Pressure
bodySections.body:
The design pressure is straightforward: users want applications to be composable and programmable, but they also want sensitive data to stay sensitive.

Confidential execution emerges when privacy is treated as infrastructure instead of a special-case feature layered on later.

quizQuestions.prompt: What tension pushes networks toward confidential execution?
Which limitation of public chains is highlighted in this lesson?
choices: They struggle to handle private state natively
